claimText: In Lithium-ion batteries, lithium ions move between a graphite negative electrode and a lithium transitional metal oxide positive electrode through a non-aqueous electrolyte during charging and discharging.
evidenceExcerpt: In Li-ion batteries, lithium ions move between a graphite negative electrode and a Li transitional metal oxide positive electrode via a non-aqueous electrolyte.
examUse: Statement-based question on the working mechanism and components of Li-ion batteries.
distractorHooks: Graphite as positive electrode instead of negative
Lead or Cadmium electrodes instead of Li transitional metal oxides

claimText: Lithium-ion batteries are characterized by high energy density, lightweight properties, lower toxic heavy metals, and generally no memory effect.
evidenceExcerpt: Li-ion batteries offer high energy density, lightweight properties, and generally no memory effect compared to older lead-acid batteries.
examUse: Comparison question between Li-ion and traditional batteries.

claimText: Black mass is a dark, powdery substance recovered from end-of-life lithium-ion batteries that contains critical minerals such as lithium, cobalt, and nickel.
evidenceExcerpt: Black mass is a dark, powdery substance recovered from end-of-life lithium-ion batteries. Black mass recovery extracts critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and nickel
examUse: Definition or correct-pair question identifying Black Mass in the context of e-waste and battery recycling.
distractorHooks: A substance found in nuclear reactors
Contains only carbon and no critical minerals

claimText: The Technology Development Board (TDB) provides financial support for the commercialization of indigenous dual-mode black mass recovery technology.
evidenceExcerpt: The Technology Development Board (TDB) is supporting indigenous dual-mode (wet and dry) black mass recovery technology.
examUse: Institution-feature question regarding the role of TDB in promoting indigenous battery recycling technologies.
distractorHooks: Supported by NITI Aayog instead of TDB
Foreign technology import instead of indigenous technology
